beats.se05: {HERO} signs the camera faulty and closes the nine-year ticket; the snow goes on unwatched; {ROUTE_A_CHAR} says nothing, and the shape inside the {SE_STATIC} keeps its shift alone while the new hire looks at easier screens

beats.se01: {ROUTE_A_CHAR} seats {HERO} at the far end of the {ROUTE_A_PLACE} in the {ANNEX} watch room, forty live monitors and one dead grey square; {ROUTE_A_CHAR} recites the watch rules while the clocks tick past 11:52 and the building comes awake: {BADGE} on the left breast face out all shift, if a corridor goes {DEADLINE_SIGN} you sit still until it whitens, and you never write on the log what the cameras will not save

beats.ma35: {HERO} cuts the {RECKONING} subsystem out of the old bus and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} sweeps stop for good; the building will not hunt the {COLD_ROOM} again, but it will not find {GHOST_NAME} either, the one who was almost found left forever unsearched-for in a room no system now remembers to keep; {ROUTE_C_CHAR} calls it peace and does not sound sure
beats.ma36: {HERO} seals the {COLD_ROOM} door and leaves every system set as they came, the {MA_COAT} folded, the {BADGE} face down, the {MA_CLIMATE} humming its long vigil on; at 1:14 next {SHIFT_NAME} the clocks will lose their eleven minutes again and the {RECKONING} will start once more toward the room; the {ANNEX} keeps what it keeps, and the new hire clocks out having changed nothing but herself
beats.ma32: the {COLD_ROOM} panel lays four last acts before {HERO} and {ROUTE_C_CHAR} stands aside to let the new hire decide how the {ANNEX} answers the man it has kept: cut the {MA_CLIMATE} and let the room finally go cold, set {GHOST_NAME}'s fallen {BADGE} upright and facing out, patch the {RECKONING} subsystem so the building stops its endless searching, or seal the door and leave every system exactly as found
beats.ma31: {ROUTE_C_CHAR} steps back from the panel and tells {HERO} the systems will answer to whatever she does here, that a new pair of hands sometimes settles a thing his own never could, and that dawn is twenty minutes off
beats.ma30: near 5:20 the {DEADLINE_SIGN} count reaches {SEALED_FLOOR} and stalls at the doorway, the building arriving at the room it has been counting toward all night and finding, as always, the man it kept but cannot account for
beats.ma29: he finds the maintenance log's one honest line, decades old, in a hand like his own: keeps a man warm, do not ask, do not stop, and beneath it a column of initials agreeing down the years
beats.ma28: {ROUTE_C_CHAR} traces the {MA_CLIMATE} feed to a single thermostat that has never once been allowed to fail, wired around three generations of upgrades so no one could switch it off by accident
beats.ma27: the desk holds a mug gone to dust, a shift roster from a year {ROUTE_C_CHAR} was a boy, and a sign-out sheet with every name struck through but one, the pen still uncapped beside it
beats.ma26: {HERO} keeps her hands to herself and reads the room instead; the {BADGE} on the floor names {GHOST_NAME}, and the panel shows the {ANNEX} has run this cold vigil since the {SHIFT_NAME} he did not clock out
beats.ma24: the {MA_COAT} is folded as if its owner meant to be back within the hour, and the fallen {BADGE} reads face inward, the sign that says do not speak to me; {HERO} feels the pull to lift the {MA_COAT} and learn the name stitched in its collar, though {ROUTE_C_CHAR} has told her twice to touch nothing on the {COLD_ROOM} that the building is still keeping
beats.ma23: {ROUTE_C_CHAR} reads the {ROUTE_A_PLACE} panel and goes quiet; the {MA_CLIMATE} has run for this room's comfort for years, the {ANNEX} keeping a post warm for someone the {AR_LEDGER} lost, and the {DEADLINE_SIGN} count is the building walking the halls looking for him
beats.ma22: the air inside is wrong in its rightness, room temperature in a building that runs cold, the one comfortable place in the whole dark {ANNEX} and no one alive meant to be warmed by it
beats.ma21: the door opens on a small {MA_CLIMATE}-perfect room the plans deny, a chair squared to a desk, a folded {MA_COAT}, and a {BADGE} on the floor lying face inward
beats.ma20: {SUPERVISOR} takes a long moment on the radio and then authorizes it in a voice that has said yes to this before, and tells {HERO} only to leave the room the way she finds it if she can
beats.ma18: the {COLD_ROOM} door is iced at its edges and the {MA_CLIMATE} line runs into it like a fed vein; the rule is plain, no {SEALED_FLOOR} unless a supervisor sends you, and no one has sent them; {ROUTE_C_CHAR} is reaching for the handle out of pure momentum, and {HERO} can force the door with him now, or make him stop and radio {SUPERVISOR} for the authorization the rule demands
beats.ma17: descending, they pass the SL1 and SL2 landings where the stair lights hold {DEADLINE_SIGN}, and {ROUTE_C_CHAR} stops them dead on each step until the {DEADLINE_SIGN} whitens
beats.ma16: it is past 3:00 now and the {MA_ELEVATOR} answers nothing, exactly as {ROUTE_C_CHAR} warned; they take the switchback stairs down toward the {COLD_ROOM} with the draw singing in the conduit beside them
beats.ma15: the thermostat spec, when {ROUTE_C_CHAR} finds it, is set to a living body's comfort, not a store's preservation, tuned for a person and not a thing
beats.ma14: the maintenance history for that line runs decades deep, the same three technicians signing off the {COLD_ROOM} {MA_CLIMATE} year on year, none ever writing down what it keeps comfortable
beats.ma13: {HERO} resets the {MA_BREAKER} the slow correct way and the trace holds; with power steady, {ROUTE_C_CHAR} runs the {MA_CLIMATE} line straight down the schematic to a single sealed door on {SEALED_FLOOR}
beats.ma11: the surge has kicked the SL2 {MA_BREAKER} and the trace stalls until it is reset; {ROUTE_C_CHAR} is already three floors ahead in his head and tells {HERO} to just jump it fast so they do not lose the thread, though the panel is old and jumping a {MA_BREAKER} on this bus is exactly the shortcut the safety card forbids
beats.ma10: at 1:14 the generator surges and every clock in the {ROUTE_C_PLACE} skips eleven minutes as one; {ROUTE_C_CHAR} says it happens every {SHIFT_NAME} and trips the same tired {MA_BREAKER} on Sub-Level 2 each time
beats.ma09: the conduit hums a low even note {ROUTE_C_CHAR} calls the {RECKONING} itself riding the old copper, a sound he says he has learned to sleep through
beats.ma08: {ROUTE_C_CHAR} shows her the load chart, the {COLD_ROOM} draw dwarfing every real tenant system, a comfort bill for a room the building swears is empty
beats.ma07: the {RECKONING} wiring is spliced into the fire panel, the door locks, and the clocks, older than any of them, so that whatever tallies the building also holds its doors and keeps its time
beats.ma06: {HERO} follows {ROUTE_C_CHAR} along the conduit, and he reads the {DEADLINE_SIGN} count off the old bus like a meter, a subsystem that wakes each {SHIFT_NAME} and tallies something the manuals never named
beats.ma04: the {COLD_ROOM} is heating and cooling a room the building calls empty, and the bill for it rises with the {DEADLINE_SIGN} count; {ROUTE_C_CHAR}, watching {HERO}'s face, offers her an easy out, he can sign her off early to the break room and handle the strange draw himself, or she can stay on her feet and help him trace where the power actually goes
beats.ma03: {ROUTE_C_CHAR} stops at a metering panel and shows her the wrong number, {SEALED_FLOOR} drawing {MA_CLIMATE} power all night though the logs swear it is empty, and the draw climbing each time the corridors overhead go {DEADLINE_SIGN}
beats.ma02: the {ROUTE_C_PLACE} is a museum of upgrades stacked on upgrades, {ROUTE_C_CHAR} says, forty years of engineers who each left the strange parts alone; he points out the panels nobody has ever been cleared to open and the ones that open themselves at 1:14
beats.ma01: {ROUTE_C_CHAR} walks {HERO} the {ROUTE_C_PLACE} of the {ANNEX} at a half-run, generator, chillers, water, and the old power bus, talking the whole way; his rules come fast, after 3:00 the {MA_ELEVATOR} answers no button so you take the stairs, and you do not go to {SEALED_FLOOR}, the {COLD_ROOM}, unless a supervisor sends you
beats.n03: {SUPERVISOR} reads the roster and names the three posts the {ANNEX} needs filled tonight, each with its own lead and its own way of seeing what the building does after dark: Security under {ROUTE_A_CHAR} at the {ROUTE_A_PLACE}, watching what the cameras refuse to record; Archives under {ROUTE_B_CHAR} in the {ROUTE_B_PLACE}, keeping cards for events not yet happened or long erased; and Maintenance under {ROUTE_C_CHAR} in the {ROUTE_C_PLACE}, tending systems that serve something besides the tenants; {HERO} picks where she stands
beats.n02: the night supervisor, {SUPERVISOR}, twelve years on the job and calm about all of it, runs {HERO} through induction: {BADGE} face out or you are not to be spoken to, stand still in any corridor that goes {DEADLINE_SIGN}, stay off {SEALED_FLOOR} unless sent, and mind that after 3:00 the {MA_ELEVATOR} stops answering; then he sorts the new hires
beats.n01: {HERO} reports for a first {SHIFT_NAME} at the {ANNEX}, a records-and-holdings complex with an unlisted address that stands locked and lightless by day and only comes awake from 11:00 at night to 5:44 before dawn; the doors will not open early, and everyone works the whole shift through

titles.se18: The Only Place He Still Exists
titles.ar50: A Record That Would Not Settle
